title: mentha × rotundifolia subsp. insularis
intro: Mentha × rotundifolia subsp. insularis, also known as Corsican mint or insular mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant, primarily native to the Mediterranean region and the island of Corsica. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include being a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Mentha × rotundifolia subsp. insularis include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing headaches, as well as having antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
